intitule: Lesson 13: Reported Questions - Understanding the Rules

enonce.texte: Read each direct question carefully. Identify the correct transformation into reported speech. Remember to apply tense backshift, change pronouns, and adjust the word order (subject before verb).

questions.texte: Direct: "Where do you live?" she asked me.
questions.reponse: She asked me where I lived.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked me where do I live.
She asked me where I lived.
She asked me where did I live.

questions.texte: Direct: "Did you see the match yesterday?" he asked his friend.
questions.reponse: He asked his friend if/whether he had seen the match the day before.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked his friend if he saw the match yesterday.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (no backshift for past simple → past perfect)

sousQuestions.texte: He asked his friend if he had seen the match the day before.
He asked his friend did he see the match the day before.

questions.texte: Direct: "Can you help me with this exercise?" the student asked.
questions.reponse: The student asked if I could help him/her with that exercise.

sousQuestions.texte: The student asked if I can help him with this exercise.
The student asked if I could help him with that exercise.
The student asked could I help him with that exercise.

intitule: Lesson 13: Reported Questions - Real-Life Application in Cameroon

enonce.texte: You are a journalist for a student newspaper in Yaoundé. You interviewed several people about education, civic duties, and health. Rewrite the direct questions from your interview into reported speech as if you were writing an article.

questions.texte: The Principal asked a student: "Why were you absent from the civic education class last week?"
questions.reponse: The Principal asked the student why he/she had been absent from the civic education class the week before.

sousQuestions.texte: The Principal asked the student why he was absent from the civic education class last week.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (past continuous → past perfect continuous; last week → the week before)

sousQuestions.texte: The Principal asked the student why had he been absent from the civic education class the week before.
The Principal asked the student why he/she had been absent from the civic education class the week before.

questions.texte: A health worker asked a patient: "Have you taken your malaria medication regularly since Monday?"
questions.reponse: A health worker asked the patient if he/she had taken his/her malaria medication regularly since Monday.

sousQuestions.texte: A health worker asked the patient if he had taken his malaria medication regularly since Monday.
sousQuestions.reponse: Correct (present perfect → past perfect; 'since Monday' often remains if still relevant)

sousQuestions.texte: A health worker asked the patient have you taken your malaria medication regularly since Monday.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (no introductory verb, no backshift, no 'if')

sousQuestions.texte: A health worker asked the patient if he took his malaria medication regularly since Monday.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (present perfect → past perfect, not past simple)

questions.texte: During a PTA meeting, a parent asked the teacher: "Will the school organize extra classes for the Probatoire examination next month?"
questions.reponse: A parent asked the teacher if the school would organize extra classes for the Probatoire examination the following month.

sousQuestions.texte: A parent asked the teacher if the school will organize extra classes for the Probatoire examination next month.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (will → would; next month → the following month)

sousQuestions.texte: A parent asked the teacher would the school organize extra classes for the Probatoire examination the following month.
A parent asked the teacher if the school would organize extra classes for the Probatoire examination the following month.

intitule: Lesson 13: Reported Questions - Mixed Tenses & WH- Words

enonce.texte: Apply all the rules: backshift, pronoun change, time/place adjustment, and correct word order. Some questions contain modal verbs or the present perfect.

questions.texte: Direct: "What were you doing when the conflict started in the neighborhood?" the police officer asked the witness.
questions.reponse: The police officer asked the witness what he/she had been doing when the conflict had started in the neighborhood.

sousQuestions.texte: The police officer asked the witness what he was doing when the conflict started.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (past continuous → past perfect continuous; past simple → past perfect)

sousQuestions.texte: The police officer asked the witness what he/she had been doing when the conflict had started in the neighborhood.
The police officer asked the witness what had he been doing when the conflict started.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect (inversion not removed and missing backshift for 'started')

questions.texte: Direct: "How long have you been using this online learning platform?" the ICT teacher asked the student.
questions.reponse: The ICT teacher asked the student how long he/she had been using that online learning platform.

sousQuestions.texte: The ICT teacher asked the student how long he had been using this online learning platform.
The ICT teacher asked the student how long he/she had been using that online learning platform.
sousQuestions.reponse: Correct (present perfect continuous → past perfect continuous)

sousQuestions.texte: The ICT teacher asked the student how long is he using that online learning platform.

questions.texte: Direct: "Must we complete the voter registration form before the deadline?" the young citizen asked the official.
questions.reponse: The young citizen asked the official if they had to complete the voter registration form before the deadline.

sousQuestions.texte: The young citizen asked the official if they must complete the voter registration form before the deadline.
The young citizen asked the official if they had to complete the voter registration form before the deadline.
The young citizen asked the official must they complete the voter registration form before the deadline.
